knownIssues: This package's nodes.py does `os.mkdir(exp_data_dir)` (not os.makedirs) at import time, unconditionally assuming ComfyUI's output directory already exists as a real directory. This is an upstream packaging bug unrelated to XPU/CUDA and not touched by Intel's extension_comfyui-advancedliveportrait.patch — but it surfaces specifically in a fresh, ephemeral runtime=docker container that copies in a ComfyUI checkout that has never actually been launched before (no output/ directory has ever been created on disk), causing a FileNotFoundError that aborts this node's registration entirely.

workarounds.action: Ensure the ComfyUI output directory (and input directory, for similar node packages) exists BEFORE the real ComfyUI process starts — e.g. `docker exec <container> mkdir -p /comfyui/outputs /comfyui/input` right after the container is created/copy-in'd but before the final `docker start`, per the Step 05 docker-runtime skill.
workarounds.rationale: Operational fix at the container-launch layer, not a source patch — avoids modifying third-party node code for what is fundamentally an environment-setup ordering issue, and defensively benefits any other node with a similar assumption.
workarounds.tradeoff: Only relevant to runtime=docker nodes with the fresh-copy-in model; bare-metal comfyui_root checkouts that have run at least once already have this directory and never hit the bug.

validationEvidence: Reproduced live: a fresh runtime=docker container copy-in of the full custom_nodes bundle failed to register ComfyUI-AdvancedLivePortrait's nodes with FileNotFoundError: '/comfyui/outputs/exp_data' during llm-scaler-omni bundle adoption Phase 2 smoke testing; confirmed root cause via traceback (nodes.py line 531, os.mkdir not os.makedirs) (2026-07-22).

provenance.taskOrigin: llm-scaler-omni bundle adoption (Phase 2 full-parity node install, container smoke test)

retireCondition: PowerHouseMan/ComfyUI-AdvancedLivePortrait changes nodes.py to os.makedirs(exp_data_dir, exist_ok=True) upstream, or the migration-agent's own Step 05 docker-runtime flow permanently bakes output-directory pre-creation into every launch (in which case this recipe becomes redundant documentation, not an active workaround).
